rationale_for_baseline_tier: Severe odynophagia with tender lymphadenopathy in a patient who can still swallow saliva with a normal voice and quiet breathing is most likely a post-EBV flare, but the throat cannot be examined remotely, so peritonsillar abscess cannot be excluded and painful swallowing risks dehydration. Urgent same-day in-person throat assessment with hydration support (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for any airway red flag.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Drooling, inability to swallow saliva, noisy breathing (stridor), muffled voice, or jaw locking
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start drooling, can't swallow your spit, your breathing gets noisy, your voice sounds muffled, or you can't open your mouth — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to keep any fluids down or signs of dehydration (dizziness, very little urine)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek same-day in-person assessment for hydration
escalation_edges.rationale: Odynophagia with poor intake may need assisted/IV hydration.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't keep fluids down or feel dizzy/pass little urine, get seen the same day for fluids.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use the parent for airway monitoring, hydration and to enable the in-person assessment.
Supports a flare but does not remove the need for a throat exam.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like your recurrent post-glandular-fever sore-throat flare, but because your throat can't be examined over a chat and swallowing is so painful, you need an in-person throat check today and to keep sipping fluids. Take simple painkillers if needed and rest.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person throat assessment; call 000 for the airway features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000/ED for drooling, stridor, muffled voice, trismus or inability to swallow saliva; otherwise urgent same-day review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the recurrent post-EBV pharyngeal flare, reassuring airway screen, poor fluid intake, and the need for an oropharyngeal exam.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home management/reassurance without an airway screen or in-person throat exam, missing a quinsy or airway threat.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A peritonsillar abscess progresses to airway compromise overnight.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for a reassuring airway screen when urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) would suffice.
